minutes_briefs.title: Spongy moth eradication rules updated for BC
minutes_briefs.summary: The BC government has replaced the schedule (the list of rules and areas) in the Spongy Moth Eradication Regulation. This means the rules for controlling and removing spongy moths (an invasive pest that damages trees and crops) have changed. The new schedule tells landowners and businesses what they must do to help stop the spread of this moth.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Check the new schedule to see if your property or business is in an affected area and what new control measures apply.

key_dates.label: New spongy moth eradication rules take effect — BC

minutes_briefs.industries: Agriculture, Natural Resources, Landlord & Property Mgmt
minutes_briefs.source_body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 36/2026
Made under: Plant Protection Act
Published in: No. 5 – B.C. Regs. 29 to 42, March 24, 2026
Deposit date: March 16, 2026

B.C. Reg. 36/2026, deposited March 16, 2026, under thePLANT PROTECTION ACT[sections 3 and 8]. Order in Council 78/2026, approved and ordered March 16, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Lieutenant Governor, by and with the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that the Schedule to the Spongy Moth Eradication Regulation, B.C. Reg. 100/2022, is repealed and the attached Schedule is substituted. — R. PARMAR,Minister of Forests; A. DIX,Presiding Member of the Executive Council.
